mnemonics.title: Nielsen's 10 Heuristics Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: Use the phrase: 'Very Few Creative Users Should Experience Nausea' - V(Visibility) F(Few=Feedback) C(Control) U(Consistency) S(Standards/Error prevention) E(Recall) N(Navigation/Flexibility) A(Aesthetic) H(Help) E(Errors) R(Recovery)

mnemonics.title: Design Thinking Phases
mnemonics.content: Remember: 'Every Designer Ignorantly Proposes Terrible Ideas' - Empathize, Define, Ideate, Prototype, Test. Alternative: 'EDIPT' sounds like 'Addict' - Everyone Does It Please Try!

mnemonics.title: Five Planes of UX
mnemonics.content: Remember: 'Super Skinny Stingrays Swim Swiftly' - Strategy, Scope, Structure, Skeleton, Surface. Or: 'S.S.S.S.S' - Same Sequence Shows Structure.

mnemonics.title: Three Pillars of UCD
mnemonics.content: Think: 'Early Empiricals Iterate' - Early focus, Empirical measurement, Iterative design

mnemonics.title: Usability Components
mnemonics.content: Remember: '3 Es of Usability' - Effectiveness (can they do it?), Efficiency (how fast?), Satisfaction (do they enjoy it?)

acronyms.expansion: User Experience - the overall experience of a user with a product/service
acronyms.usage: Used to describe holistic view of user interaction including emotions, perceptions, and journey

acronyms.expansion: User-Centered Design - design approach centered on user needs
acronyms.usage: Philosophy guiding design decisions based on user requirements

acronyms.expansion: Human-Computer Interaction - study of human interaction with computers
acronyms.usage: Academic field underlying UX and interface design

acronyms.expansion: Interaction Design - design of interactive behaviors
acronyms.usage: Creating how users interact with products
Creating structure for websites and applications

acronyms.expansion: Graphical User Interface - visual interface users interact with
acronyms.usage: The visual elements users see and interact with

acronyms.expansion: User Interface - point of human-computer interaction
acronyms.usage: The means by which users interact with a system

acronyms.expansion: System Usability Scale - 10-question survey for usability
acronyms.usage: Standard questionnaire to measure perceived usability

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a pyramid with Surface (top/visible) as the tip, then Skeleton (framework), then Structure (organization), then Scope (requirements), with Strategy (bottom/foundation) as the base. Each plane builds on the one below.
Picture a circle going: Empathize → Define → Ideate → Prototype → Test → (back to Empathize). It flows continuously, not linearly.
Draw overlapping circles: Usability (task-focused), Usefulness (value), Desirability (emotional appeal), Accessibility (inclusive), Credible (trust). Where they all overlap = User Experience.
Create a checklist poster with all 10 items, use bright colors for common ones (Visibility, Error Prevention, Help). Place near your workstation.

analogies.analogy: Like how a car's basic function (transportation = usability) expanded to include comfort, style, safety features, and driving experience (UX). A car still needs to drive, but the experience matters too.
Like an actor playing a character role - the designer 'becomes' the persona to understand their perspective, motivations, and pain points when making design decisions.
Like a library's cataloging system - how books are organized, labeled with signs, and arranged so visitors can find what they need efficiently.
Like sculpting - you don't carve the final form in one attempt. You chip away, evaluate, adjust, and refine until you achieve the desired result.
Like a flight simulator - it tests how users (pilots) would perform in realistic scenarios without real-world risks, identifying training (design) needs.
